arabic: قٓۚ وَٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ ٱلۡمَجِيدِ
transliteration: Qaf waalqurani almajeedi
translation: Qaf. By the Glorious Qur'aan, [We swear its authenticity]!
notes: This is one of the letters of the Arabic language that appear at the beginning of some Qur'aanic chapters. Significance of these letters is a mystery. Omniscient Allah has perhaps purposely kept it mysterious to remind mankind of the limitation of knowledge it is bestowed with.

arabic: بَلۡ عَجِبُوٓاْ أَن جَآءَهُم مُّنذِرٞ مِّنۡهُمۡ فَقَالَ ٱلۡكَٰفِرُونَ هَٰذَا شَيۡءٌ عَجِيبٌ
transliteration: Bal AAajiboo an jaahum munthirun minhum faqala alkafiroona hatha shayon AAajeebun
translation: But they wonder that there has come to them a Warner [from Allah] from among themselves. So, the suppressors of the Truth say, "This is a strange thing!"

arabic: أَءِذَا مِتۡنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابٗاۖ ذَٰلِكَ رَجۡعُۢ بَعِيدٞ
transliteration: Aitha mitna wakunna turaban thalika rajAAun baAAeedun
translation: "What! When we die and have become dust, (shall we rise again to life?) That is a return far [from being ever possible]!"

arabic: قَدۡ عَلِمۡنَا مَا تَنقُصُ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ مِنۡهُمۡۖ وَعِندَنَا كِتَٰبٌ حَفِيظُۢ
transliteration: Qad AAalimna ma tanqusu alardu minhum waAAindana kitabun hafeethun
translation: We do know what the earth takes away from them. And with Us is a record protected!

arabic: بَلۡ كَذَّبُواْ بِٱلۡحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمۡ فَهُمۡ فِيٓ أَمۡرٖ مَّرِيجٍ
transliteration: Bal kaththaboo bialhaqqi lamma jaahum fahum fee amrin mareejin
translation: But they deny the Truth when it comes to them. And so, they are in a confused state of mind.

arabic: أَفَلَمۡ يَنظُرُوٓاْ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَوۡقَهُمۡ كَيۡفَ بَنَيۡنَٰهَا وَزَيَّنَّـٰهَا وَمَا لَهَا مِن فُرُوجٖ
transliteration: Afalam yanthuroo ila alssamai fawqahum kayfa banaynaha wazayyannaha wama laha min furoojin
translation: Do they not look at the sky above them – how We have made it and adorned it, and see that there are no cracks in it?

arabic: وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ مَدَدۡنَٰهَا وَأَلۡقَيۡنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِيَ وَأَنۢبَتۡنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوۡجِۭ بَهِيجٖ
transliteration: Waalarda madadnaha waalqayna feeha rawasiya waanbatna feeha min kulli zawjin baheejin
translation: And We have spread out the earth and cast mountains into it. And We have caused every kind of beautiful spouse of herbage grow in it.

arabic: تَبۡصِرَةٗ وَذِكۡرَىٰ لِكُلِّ عَبۡدٖ مُّنِيبٖ
transliteration: Tabsiratan wathikra likulli AAabdin muneebin
translation: To be observed and commemorated by every devotee turning to Allah.

arabic: وَنَزَّلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ مُّبَٰرَكٗا فَأَنۢبَتۡنَا بِهِۦ جَنَّـٰتٖ وَحَبَّ ٱلۡحَصِيدِ
transliteration: Wanazzalna mina alssamai maan mubarakan faanbatna bihi jannatin wahabba alhaseedi
translation: And We send down from the sky rain as a blessing, and We produce therewith gardens and seed for the harvest

arabic: وَٱلنَّخۡلَ بَاسِقَٰتٖ لَّهَا طَلۡعٞ نَّضِيدٞ
transliteration: Waalnnakhla basiqatin laha talAAun nadeedun
translation: And tall palm-trees, with shoots of fruit-stalks, piled one over another

arabic: رِّزۡقٗا لِّلۡعِبَادِۖ وَأَحۡيَيۡنَا بِهِۦ بَلۡدَةٗ مَّيۡتٗاۚ كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلۡخُرُوجُ
transliteration: Rizqan lilAAibadi waahyayna bihi baldatan maytan kathalika alkhurooju
translation: A provision for (Allah's) subjects. And We give life therewith [with rain] to land that is dead. Thus, will the coming out of the dead from their graves [Resurrection] be.

arabic: كَذَّبَتۡ قَبۡلَهُمۡ قَوۡمُ نُوحٖ وَأَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلرَّسِّ وَثَمُودُ
transliteration: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin waashabu alrrassi wathamoodu
translation: [Resurrection] was denied before them by the People of Noah , the Companions of the Rass , and the Thamood ,
notes: These as well as those mentioned in the next two Verses are all Peoples of pre-historic ages.

arabic: وَعَادٞ وَفِرۡعَوۡنُ وَإِخۡوَٰنُ لُوطٖ
transliteration: WaAAadun wafirAAawnu waikhwanu lootin
translation: And of AAad, of Pharaoh, the brethren of Lot,

arabic: وَأَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلۡأَيۡكَةِ وَقَوۡمُ تُبَّعٖۚ كُلّٞ كَذَّبَ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ
transliteration: Waashabu alaykati waqawmu tubbaAAin kullun kaththaba alrrusula fahaqqa waAAeedi
translation: The Companions of the Wood, and the People of Tubba' (an ancient tribe) – all of them rejected the Messengers, and My warning (to them) was duly fulfilled.

arabic: أَفَعَيِينَا بِٱلۡخَلۡقِ ٱلۡأَوَّلِۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ فِي لَبۡسٖ مِّنۡ خَلۡقٖ جَدِيدٖ
transliteration: AfaAAayeena bialkhalqi alawwali bal hum fee labsin min khalqin jadeedin
translation: Were We then weary with the first Creation that they should be in such a confused state of mind about a new Creation!?

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنَ وَنَعۡلَمُ مَا تُوَسۡوِسُ بِهِۦ نَفۡسُهُۥۖ وَنَحۡنُ أَقۡرَبُ إِلَيۡهِ مِنۡ حَبۡلِ ٱلۡوَرِيدِ
transliteration: Walaqad khalaqna alinsana wanaAAlamu ma tuwaswisu bihi nafsuhu wanahnu aqrabu ilayhi min habli alwareedi
translation: And We did create man, and We know the promptings of his own self. And We are closer to him than any bodily channel having access into his brain.
notes: Brain is the seat where all human thoughts arise. There is an intricate network of vessels therein connected to other parts of the body like the heart. The connecting vessels to parts below the neck have necessarily to pass through the neck. The connecting vessels in the neck are therefore the most prominent of such vessels that have closest connections with the brain, the seat of all human thought. The Verse here refers to any of such vessels. But in translations, the Arabic term, habli alwareed , is represented by the jugular vein, because of the prominence of the neck vessel. The Verse tells us that the divine forces are closer even to such vessels going closest to the seat of human thought.

arabic: إِذۡ يَتَلَقَّى ٱلۡمُتَلَقِّيَانِ عَنِ ٱلۡيَمِينِ وَعَنِ ٱلشِّمَالِ قَعِيدٞ
transliteration: Ith yatalaqqa almutalaqqiyani AAani alyameeni waAAani alshshimali qaAAeedun
translation: When the two frequently confronting voices, one located on the right and one on the left, confront each other
notes: Man has frequently to make a choice between two conflicting urges arising in his mind. To give an example from real life published in newspapers a few years ago, a woman found out that her young son had committed a crime. Her instinct was to hide this fact and save her son from punishment he was liable to under the law. But she was an Allah-fearing woman. And her conscience told her that this world's punishment is better than the punishment in the Hereafter. So, she herself took her son to the authorities that day! It is such conflicting views arising in a human mind that this Verse is alluding to here.

arabic: مَّا يَلۡفِظُ مِن قَوۡلٍ إِلَّا لَدَيۡهِ رَقِيبٌ عَتِيدٞ
transliteration: Ma yalfithu min qawlin illa ladayhi raqeebun AAateedun
translation: He utters not a word but is there a sentinel by him, ready [to record it]!

arabic: وَجَآءَتۡ سَكۡرَةُ ٱلۡمَوۡتِ بِٱلۡحَقِّۖ ذَٰلِكَ مَا كُنتَ مِنۡهُ تَحِيدُ
transliteration: Wajaat sakratu almawti bialhaqqi thalika ma kunta minhu taheedu
translation: And the pangs of death will bring the Truth home to him. "That was the thing you kept away from!"

arabic: وَنُفِخَ فِي ٱلصُّورِۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوۡمُ ٱلۡوَعِيدِ
transliteration: Wanufikha fee alssoori thalika yawmu alwaAAeedi
translation: And then the Trumpet is blown – there then is the Day promised!

arabic: وَجَآءَتۡ كُلُّ نَفۡسٖ مَّعَهَا سَآئِقٞ وَشَهِيدٞ
transliteration: Wajaat kullu nafsin maAAaha saiqun washaheedun
translation: And every soul comes forth accompanied by a driver and a witness.

arabic: لَّقَدۡ كُنتَ فِي غَفۡلَةٖ مِّنۡ هَٰذَا فَكَشَفۡنَا عَنكَ غِطَآءَكَ فَبَصَرُكَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ حَدِيدٞ
transliteration: Laqad kunta fee ghaflatin min hatha fakashafna AAanka ghitaaka fabasaruka alyawma hadeedun
translation: "You were careless about this. Now that We have taken off your cover from you, strong is your eyesight this Day!"

arabic: وَقَالَ قَرِينُهُۥ هَٰذَا مَا لَدَيَّ عَتِيدٌ
transliteration: Waqala qareenuhu hatha ma ladayya AAateedun
translation: And his Companion [witness] says, "It's ready here – what in my hand is [man's record]!"

arabic: أَلۡقِيَا فِي جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٖ
transliteration: Alqiya fee jahannama kulla kaffarin AAaneedin
translation: "Throw into Hell every stubborn suppressor of evidence proving existence of Allah [with attributes as given in the Qur'aan]!"

arabic: مَّنَّاعٖ لِّلۡخَيۡرِ مُعۡتَدٖ مُّرِيبٍ
transliteration: MannaAAin lilkhayri muAAtadin mureebin
translation: "Who was forbidding what was good, transgressing all bounds, and casting doubts and suspicions."

arabic: ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَأَلۡقِيَاهُ فِي ٱلۡعَذَابِ ٱلشَّدِيدِ
transliteration: Allathee jaAAala maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara faalqiyahu fee alAAathabi alshshadeedi
translation: "Who had set up other god/s besides Allah! So, throw him into the severe punishment."

arabic: ۞قَالَ قَرِينُهُۥ رَبَّنَا مَآ أَطۡغَيۡتُهُۥ وَلَٰكِن كَانَ فِي ضَلَٰلِۭ بَعِيدٖ
transliteration: Qala qareenuhu rabbana ma atghaytuhu walakin kana fee dalalin baAAeedin
translation: His Companion says, "Our Lord! I did not make him transgress, but he was (himself) gone far astray."

arabic: قَالَ لَا تَخۡتَصِمُواْ لَدَيَّ وَقَدۡ قَدَّمۡتُ إِلَيۡكُم بِٱلۡوَعِيدِ
transliteration: Qala la takhtasimoo ladayya waqad qaddamtu ilaykum bialwaAAeedi
translation: He [Allah] says, "Quarrel not in My Presence; and I did send you the warning in advance."

arabic: مَا يُبَدَّلُ ٱلۡقَوۡلُ لَدَيَّ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ بِظَلَّـٰمٖ لِّلۡعَبِيدِ
transliteration: Ma yubaddalu alqawlu ladayya wama ana bithallamin lilAAabeedi
translation: "No alteration in the Word with Me, and I am never unjust to My subjects."

arabic: يَوۡمَ نَقُولُ لِجَهَنَّمَ هَلِ ٱمۡتَلَأۡتِ وَتَقُولُ هَلۡ مِن مَّزِيدٖ
transliteration: Yawma naqoolu lijahannama hali imtalati wataqoolu hal min mazeedin
translation: That Day We ask Hell, "Are you full?" And it asks, "Are there any more?"

arabic: وَأُزۡلِفَتِ ٱلۡجَنَّةُ لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ غَيۡرَ بَعِيدٍ
transliteration: Waozlifati aljannatu lilmuttaqeena ghayra baAAeedin
translation: And the Garden [of Paradise] is brought near for the pious and righteous, and it is no longer a distant objective [for them.]

arabic: هَٰذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِكُلِّ أَوَّابٍ حَفِيظٖ
transliteration: Hatha ma tooAAadoona likulli awwabin hafeethin
translation: "This is what you were promised. [And this is what was promised] to everyone who turned (to Allah in sincere repentance) and protected (himself against satanic influence.)

arabic: مَّنۡ خَشِيَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنَ بِٱلۡغَيۡبِ وَجَآءَ بِقَلۡبٖ مُّنِيبٍ
transliteration: Man khashiya alrrahmana bialghaybi wajaa biqalbin muneebin
translation: Who feared the Gracious One without physically seeing Him with worldly eyes and has come with a mind turned in devotion (to Him).

arabic: ٱدۡخُلُوهَا بِسَلَٰمٖۖ ذَٰلِكَ يَوۡمُ ٱلۡخُلُودِ
transliteration: Odkhulooha bisalamin thalika yawmu alkhuloodi
translation: "Enter therein [in Paradise] in Peace and Security; that is time of eternal life!"

arabic: لَهُم مَّا يَشَآءُونَ فِيهَا وَلَدَيۡنَا مَزِيدٞ
transliteration: Lahum ma yashaoona feeha waladayna mazeedun
translation: There is for them therein all that they desire. And [there is besides] much more with Us.

arabic: وَكَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا قَبۡلَهُم مِّن قَرۡنٍ هُمۡ أَشَدُّ مِنۡهُم بَطۡشٗا فَنَقَّبُواْ فِي ٱلۡبِلَٰدِ هَلۡ مِن مَّحِيصٍ
transliteration: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin hum ashaddu minhum batshan fanaqqaboo fee albiladi hal min maheesin
translation: And how many a generation before them, stronger in power than they, did We destroy! They had wandered through the lands in search of a place of escape!!

arabic: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَذِكۡرَىٰ لِمَن كَانَ لَهُۥ قَلۡبٌ أَوۡ أَلۡقَى ٱلسَّمۡعَ وَهُوَ شَهِيدٞ
transliteration: Inna fee thalika lathikra liman kana lahu qalbun aw alqa alssamAAa wahuwa shaheedun
translation: Certainly indeed in that is a lesson to learn for any who has an understanding mind or who lends an insightful ear.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٖ وَمَا مَسَّنَا مِن لُّغُوبٖ
transliteration: Walaqad khalaqna alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma fee sittati ayyamin wama massana min lughoobin
translation: We created the heavens and the earth and all things in between in six periods of time. And weariness did not even touch Us!

arabic: فَٱصۡبِرۡ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِ رَبِّكَ قَبۡلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمۡسِ وَقَبۡلَ ٱلۡغُرُوبِ
transliteration: Faisbir AAala ma yaqooloona wasabbih bihamdi rabbika qabla tulooAAi alshshamsi waqabla alghuroobi
translation: Have patience, then, over what they say, and glorify your Lord with praise, before the rising of the sun and before its setting.

arabic: وَمِنَ ٱلَّيۡلِ فَسَبِّحۡهُ وَأَدۡبَٰرَ ٱلسُّجُودِ
transliteration: Wamina allayli fasabbihhu waadbara alssujoodi
translation: And glorify Him during parts of the night, and post prostrations!
notes: These two Verses tell us that we should keep remembering Allah, with all His Glory, constantly throughout the time we are awake while doing our mundane work. That is, besides our Salah timings as prescribed in Verses 17:78, 11:114 & 2:238. During Salah, of course, all mundane work – and even thought – are proscribed!

arabic: وَٱسۡتَمِعۡ يَوۡمَ يُنَادِ ٱلۡمُنَادِ مِن مَّكَانٖ قَرِيبٖ
transliteration: WaistamiAA yawma yunadi almunadi min makanin qareebin
translation: And listen the Day when the Caller calls out from a place quite near, –

arabic: يَوۡمَ يَسۡمَعُونَ ٱلصَّيۡحَةَ بِٱلۡحَقِّۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوۡمُ ٱلۡخُرُوجِ
transliteration: Yawma yasmaAAoona alssayhata bialhaqqi thalika yawmu alkhurooji
translation: The Day when they hear a real mighty Blast – that is the Day of Resurrection!

arabic: إِنَّا نَحۡنُ نُحۡيِۦ وَنُمِيتُ وَإِلَيۡنَا ٱلۡمَصِيرُ
transliteration: Inna nahnu nuhyee wanumeetu wailayna almaseeru
translation: It is indeed We Who give life and cause death; and to Us is the final destination.

arabic: يَوۡمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ عَنۡهُمۡ سِرَاعٗاۚ ذَٰلِكَ حَشۡرٌ عَلَيۡنَا يَسِيرٞ
transliteration: Yawma tashaqqaqu alardu AAanhum siraAAan thalika hashrun AAalayna yaseerun
translation: That Day the earth swiftly separates from them. That gathering – it is easy for Us!

arabic: نَّحۡنُ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيۡهِم بِجَبَّارٖۖ فَذَكِّرۡ بِٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ مَن يَخَافُ وَعِيدِ
transliteration: Nahnu aAAlamu bima yaqooloona wama anta AAalayhim bijabbarin fathakkir bialqurani man yakhafu waAAeedi
translation: We know what they say! And you are not to be coercive on them. So, admonish with the Qur'aan such as fear My Warning!
